mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 2 Series 223i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned B48 engine typically costs £3,500–£5,500 supply only, with fitting adding £1,000–£1,500. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your 223i.

Yes — the B48 is a well-established, robust engine. It's used across the BMW range and has proven reliable. The main watchpoints are the water pump and injectors — both are manageable with proper maintenance.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 223i engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Water pump failure, injector failure, and MHEV system integration issues. The engine itself is fundamentally strong and not prone to major failures.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a 223i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. An U06 223i is a £20,000–£35,000 vehicle — a £4,500 recon is 13–23% of the car's value, making replacement a clear decision. This is well within our 70% BMW scrap threshold.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 223i engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The 223i engine code is B48 — a 2.0-litre turbo petrol with 48V MHEV. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I prevent the water pump failure on a 223i?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — many specialists recommend replacing the water pump preventatively at 60,000–80,000 miles. If you notice temperature fluctuations or coolant loss, have the cooling system inspected immediately.

replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 2 Series 223i engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

problems.title: Water Pump Failure
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss, temperature fluctuations, engine warning light, heater not working properly, overheating.
problems.repairCost: £500–£1,000 (electric water pump replacement) [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]
problems.recommendation: Like other modern BMW engines, the B48 uses an electric water pump with a finite life. If you notice temperature fluctuations or coolant loss, have the cooling system inspected. Replacing the water pump preventatively at 60,000–80,000 miles is recommended by specialists.

problems.title: Injector Failure
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, misfire, fuel smell from exhaust, increased fuel consumption, engine warning light.
problems.repairCost: £600–£1,200 (injector set replacement) [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]
problems.recommendation: B48 injectors are generally reliable, but they can fail over time. Using high-quality fuel and regular service can extend their life. If you experience rough running or misfires, have the injectors tested — replacing them as a set is recommended if multiple are failing.

problems.title: MHEV System Integration Issues
problems.symptoms: Reduced power, regenerative braking issues, warning lights, start-stop not functioning correctly.
problems.repairCost: Varies — often covered under warranty; out-of-warranty can be £500–£2,000+
problems.recommendation: The 48V MHEV system is generally reliable, but the 48V battery and electric motor can experience issues. If you notice warning lights or reduced power, have the system diagnostics run. Many issues are software-related and can be resolved with updates.

rows.whenItMakesSense: Replace as a set — use latest revision
Often software — can be resolved; hardware faults require specialist
Viable on lower-mileage examples; assess overall engine health
Little left to salvage — replacement is the only option

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 223i? Check These First
buyIf: * Documented cooling system maintenance (water pump inspected/replaced)
* No signs of coolant loss or temperature fluctuations
* Full BMW service history with correct oil spec
* MHEV system functioning correctly (no warning lights)
avoidIf: * Coolant loss with no diagnosis — water pump may be failing
* Rough idle with no injector history — may indicate injection issues
* MHEV warning lights with no diagnosis — potentially expensive system faults
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis — potentially complex issue

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The B48 is a modern, well-engineered engine that powers the 223i with efficiency and refinement. The water pump and injectors are the main watchpoints — both are well-understood and manageable. The MHEV system adds complexity but is generally reliable. A well-maintained 223i is a dependable, efficient family car.

cta.label: → Compare 223i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 2 Series 223i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Coolant Level — Check for unexplained loss; water pump failure is common
* Cold Start — Listen for smooth start; B48 is generally quiet
* Service History — including engine era (B48 MHEV) and confirmed maintenance
* Rough Idle — Check for uneven running; injector issues possible
* MHEV System — Check for warning lights; ensure start-stop works correctly
* Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 1,000 miles?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
